title: She Almost Threw Away a $1 Million Lottery Ticket
script: She almost trashed it! Melissa Johnson from Hendersonville, North Carolina, bought a 10-dollar scratch-off ticket at Rock Convenience on Chimney Rock Road. She scratched it, looked it over, decided it was a loser — and set it aside to throw away. Then she thought: just scan it first. Her phone said $1 million. Johnson said her heart started beating fast. Then she started screaming. The ticket was a winner for the North Carolina Education Lottery — a top-prize 'Loteria Multiplier' ticket. She took the lump sum: $600,000 before taxes, $432,063 after. And the $10 she almost tossed into the trash is now paying for her family's future. The moral: always scan your scratch-offs before you throw them out. Unless — what if the trash can knew something you didn't?
